perturbation.original_text: c. “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this shall not be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: c. “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization *currently* renders or provides Banking and Financial Services. *Former* Customers or *Former* Customer shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this shall not be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction in how 'Customer' is defined. Initially, it encompassed any person served within one year of termination. The modified text introduces a distinction between 'Customers' and 'Former Customers', implying that the term 'Customer' only refers to current customers, but later sections still use the term 'Customer' as it included former clients. This change introduces ambiguity regarding non-solicitation clauses that restrict the employee from contacting 'Customers', creating an **in-text contradiction** on which customer are being applied the restrictions of solicitations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Customers' as anyone served within one year of termination. The changed text introduces 'Former Customers' and narrows the definition of 'Customers' to only current clients. This contradicts Section 5, which prohibits soliciting 'Customers', as it is now unclear whether this restriction applies to former clients, creating an in-text contradiction. The change directly impacts the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause, as the term 'Customer' is redefined without clarifying its application to former clients in the context of the non-solicitation agreement. This change creates ambiguity about which customers are being applied the restrictions of solicitations, therefore a strong contradiction is introduced.

perturbation.original_text: d. “Employees,” “Agents,” and “Independent Contractors” shall mean any and all persons employed or contracted by a Banking Organization or who were employed or contracted by a Banking Organization, as an employee, agent or independent contractor, within the six (6) month period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution.
perturbation.changed_text: d. “Employees,” “Agents,” and “Independent Contractors” shall mean any and all persons *currently* employed or contracted by a Banking Organization. *Former Employees,* “Agents,” and “Independent Contractors” shall mean any and all persons employed or contracted by a Banking Organization or who were employed or contracted by a Banking Organization, as an employee, agent or independent contractor, within the six (6) month period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing the term "Former Employees", and defining "Employees" as *currently* employed, create a contradiction regarding the non-solicitation agreement since the section uses term 'Employees' to apply the restriction. The **in-text contradiction** is on who is being covered on the employee soliciation restriction by the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Non-Solicitation of Employees and Others . In consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a 5 Financial Institution, and for a period of two (2) years immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: c. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any Employee to terminate his or her employment with a Banking Organization, or offer or provide employment (whether such employment is with Employee or any other Person) either on a full-time or part-time, consulting or Independent Contractor basis; or d. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any Independent Contractors or Agents to terminate their contract or relationship with a Banking Organization or discontinue providing services to a Banking Organization and/or Customers; or e. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any suppliers, vendors or others who were supplying services or goods to a Banking Organization during the one year period prior to Employee’s termination, to terminate, reduce, limit or change their business or relationship with a Banking Organization.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Employees' to include those within the six months prior to termination. The changed text creates two separate categories: 'Employees' (currently employed) and 'Former Employees' (those employed within the six months prior to termination). The 'Contradicted Text' restricts 'Employee' from soliciting other 'Employees'. Since the changed text redefines 'Employees' as only those currently employed, it creates an ambiguity/contradiction on whether the non-solicitation agreement applies to former employees, which is the core of the explanation and creates in-text contradiction based on the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that *only during the Employee's employment period* by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase "*only during the Employee's employment period*" limits the duration of when the employee has advantageous familiarity. This could conflict with later sections of the agreement that impose restrictions on the employee for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment, creating an **in-text contradiction** on when exactly the employee is subjected to those restrictions given the advantageous familiarity acquired during the employment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that *only during the Employee's employment period* by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "*only during the Employee's employment period*" in the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that the employee is subject to restrictions for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment, creating an in-text contradiction on when exactly the employee is subjected to those restrictions given the advantageous familiarity acquired during the employment. This directly conflicts with the non-solicitation obligations after termination, making the terms inconsistent and leading to a strong in-text contradiction, as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization is entitled to the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 5 and 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction will not begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. *Optional* Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization *may request* the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 5 and 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction *may not* begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the tolling of covenants optional, which contradicts the original intent of providing the Banking Organization with the full post-termination restrictions. This change introduces ambiguity by replacing mandatory terms such as 'is entitled to' with 'may request' and 'will not begin' with 'may not begin', which makes the application on the post termination restrictions optional by the organization. This creates an **internal contradiction** if another part of the contract enforces strict adherence of the post-termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. *Optional* Tolling of Covenants . Employee acknowledges that a Banking Organization *may request* the full post-termination restrictions on the activities set forth in Sections 5 and 6 , as applicable. Therefore, in the event any of the provisions of these Sections are breached by Employee, the commencement of the applicable post-termination restriction *may not* begin until Employee is in full compliance with Section 5 and/or 6 .
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the tolling of covenants, ensuring the Banking Organization received full post-termination restrictions. The changed text makes this optional ('may request', 'may not begin'), directly contradicting the intention of strict adherence. This ambiguity clashes with any clause expecting mandatory enforcement of the restrictions, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it makes post-termination restrictions optional, while other clauses may enforce them.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Severability/Blue Pencil . Each of the provisions of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the restrictive covenants in Sections 5 and 6 , are distinct and severable, notwithstanding that the covenants may be set forth in one section for convenience.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Severability/Blue Pencil . Each of the provisions of this Agreement, *except the restrictive covenants in Sections 5 and 6*, are distinct and severable, notwithstanding that the covenants may be set forth in one section for convenience.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the restrictive covenants in Sections 5 and 6 are *not* distinct and severable, it implies that if any part of these sections is deemed invalid, the entire section is invalid. This directly contradicts the general severability clause, creating an **in-text contradiction** and potentially invalidating those sections if any portion is found unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Severability/Blue Pencil . Each of the provisions of this Agreement, *except the restrictive covenants in Sections 5 and 6*, are distinct and severable, notwithstanding that the covenants may be set forth in one section for convenience. If any provision of this Agreement shall be determined to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable in whole or in part, for any reason, neither the validity of the remaining part of such provision nor the validity of any other provision of this Agreement shall in any way be affected. Should any particular restrictive covenant, provision or clause of this Agreement be held unreasonable or unenforceable for any reason, including without limitation, the time period, geographic area and/or scope of activity covered by such covenant, provision or clause, the parties acknowledge and agree that such covenant, provision or clause shall be given effect and enforce to whatever extent would be reasonable and enforceable under applicable law. The parties expressly authorize a court of competent jurisdiction to modify or revise such provision to limit the covenants to cover the maximum period of time, range of activities or other restrictions as would be enforceable under Indiana law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the restrictive covenants in Sections 5 and 6 are *not* distinct and severable. The contradicted text, which is actually the same section, attempts to apply severability to the entire agreement, including those same restrictive covenants. This creates a direct conflict because the change carves out an exception, negating the severability provision for those specific covenants. The Perturbation Explanation states that this implies that if any part of these sections is deemed invalid, the entire section is invalid, which contradicts the idea of severability as outlined in the original text and contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be interpreted under, subject to and governed by the laws of the State of Indiana, without consideration of the choice of law principles thereof, and all questions concerning its validity, construction and administration shall be determined in accordance with Indiana law.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be interpreted under, subject to and governed by the laws of *the state where the Employee resides at the time of enforcement*, without consideration of the choice of law principles thereof, and all questions concerning its validity, construction and administration shall be determined in accordance with Indiana law.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by specifying that the agreement is governed by the laws of *the state where the Employee resides at the time of enforcement* instead of Indiana law. While the construction and administration remain under Indiana law, the governing law for interpretation shifts, creating an **in-text contradiction** and uncertainty about which state's laws apply to aspects other than construction and administration, such as breach of contract claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be interpreted under, subject to and governed by the laws of *the state where the Employee resides at the time of enforcement*, without consideration of the choice of law principles thereof, and all questions concerning its validity, construction and administration shall be determined in accordance with Indiana law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from the laws of Indiana to the laws of the state where the employee resides at the time of enforcement. While it maintains that the construction and administration shall be determined in accordance with Indiana law, it creates a clear contradiction by applying different state laws for interpretation and other aspects of the agreement. This shift in governing law introduces an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about which state's laws apply, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, between the modified section and itself (Contradicted Text).
